info.title: CoolerControl Daemon API
info.description: Basic OpenAPI documentation for the CoolerControl Daemon API

Returns a Health Check Status.

400.description: Bad Request. The request is invalid

get.description: Returns running min/max/avg/count per channel and temp since daemon start. Entries are present once observed at least once.

200.description:
Top-level response for `GET /stats` and `DELETE /stats`. One entry per
 known device. Channels/temps are only present once they have been
 observed at least once since daemon start (or last reset).

delete.description: Clears all running stats and reseeds each from the most recent status with count=1. Channels absent from the most recent status are dropped and will reseed naturally on their next observation.

200.description:
Top-level response for `GET /stats` and `DELETE /stats`. One entry per
 known device. Channels/temps are only present once they have been
 observed at least once since daemon start (or last reset).

get.description: Returns one entry per channel that has a stored calibration. Used by the UI at app load to mark calibrated channels in the tree menu without one request per channel. Always 200; an empty list signals that no channel has been calibrated yet.

200.description:
Snapshot of every persisted calibration. Empty list when nothing
 is stored. Matches the wrapper shape used by `/profiles` and
 `/alerts` so clients can iterate `dto.calibrations`.

post.description: Queues a calibration diagnosis on the channel and returns 202. Poll GET .../status to track progress. Returns 409 when a diagnosis is already in flight for the channel.

400.description: Bad Request. The request is invalid

404.description: Whatever you're looking for, it's not here.

429.description: Too Many Requests. Login attempts have been rate limited.

502.description: Bad Gateway. An error has occurred with an external library.

post.description: Triggers cancellation of the in-flight calibration for the channel. Returns 404 when nothing is running.

400.description: Bad Request. The request is invalid

404.description: Whatever you're looking for, it's not here.

429.description: Too Many Requests. Login attempts have been rate limited.

502.description: Bad Gateway. An error has occurred with an external library.

get.description: Returns the most recent calibration status for the channel (in_progress / completed / failed). 404 if no diagnosis has ever been observed. Intended for UI polling at ~1 Hz while a diagnosis is in flight.

200.description:
Per-channel status of the latest calibration attempt. The UI polls
 `GET /calibrations/.../status` to drive its progress UI; the engine
 rewrites this entry on every sweep step and once more on the final
 terminal transition. The `Completed` and `Failed` variants stick
 around until the next diagnosis on the same channel resets it back
 to `InProgress`. `NotStarted` is returned when neither an in-memory
 snapshot nor a persisted calibration exists for the channel; the
 endpoint returns 200 in that case so the UI does not have to
 distinguish "channel unknown" from network errors via HTTP 404.

400.description: Bad Request. The request is invalid

404.description: Whatever you're looking for, it's not here.

429.description: Too Many Requests. Login attempts have been rate limited.

502.description: Bad Gateway. An error has occurred with an external library.

patch.description: Replaces the kick-boost and kick-duration override fields on the stored calibration. Both fields in the body are applied unconditionally; `null` clears the override. Returns 404 when no calibration is stored for the channel.

requestBody.description:
Per-fan calibration override values. `null` clears the override
 and falls back to the auto-derived behavior (heuristic for the
 boost, calibrated `kick_duration_ms` for the duration, walk-down
 enabled by default).

200.description:
Calibration as exposed via the REST API. Flattens the persistence
 struct and adds derived fields the UI consumes.

get.description: Subscribes and returns the Server Sent Events for a Log stream
Subscribes and returns the Server Sent Events for a Status stream
Subscribes and returns the Server Sent Events for a ModeActivated stream
Subscribes and returns Events for when an Alert State has changed
Subscribes and returns Events for desktop notifications that should be displayed to the user

CookieAuth.name: cc
CookieAuth.description: The private session cookie used for authentication.

BasicAuth.description: HTTP Basic authentication, mostly used to generate a secure authentication cookie.

BearerAuth.description: Bearer token authentication for external services.

desktop_notify.description: Toggle a desktop notification when this alert enters an `Active` state. (true by default)

desktop_notify_audio.description:
Toggle whether the desktop notification attempts to play an audio sound
 when this alert enters an `Active` state.
 Note: only applies when `desktop_notify` is enabled.

desktop_notify_recovery.description: Toggle a desktop notification when this alert enters an `Inactive` state.

shutdown_on_activation.description:
Toggle whether to issue a system shutdown when this Alert enters an `Active` state.
 Duration of the alert before shutdown is determined by `shutdown_active_duration`.

warmup_duration.description: Time in seconds throughout which the alert condition must hold before the alert is activated

oneOf.description:
Alert condition was satisfied at the stored time
 but the duration threshold has not been reached.
Represents an error state. e.g. when one of the components in the alert isn't found.

extension.description: Specialized settings (extensions) that apply to a specific device channel.

CCDeviceSettings.description: Device Specific settings that generally apply to how the application deals with the device.

channel_settings.description: A list of channels specific settings, including disable and extension settings.

disable.description: All communication with this device will be avoided if disabled

extensions.description: Specialized settings (extensions) that apply to a specific device.

name.description: The device name for this setting. Helpful after blacklisting(disabling) devices.

CalibrationEntryView.description:
Same wire shape as `crate::calibration::CalibrationEntry`, with
 the wrapped view in place of the bare calibration.

CalibrationOverridesUpdate.description:
Per-fan calibration override values. `null` clears the override
 and falls back to the auto-derived behavior (heuristic for the
 boost, calibrated `kick_duration_ms` for the duration, walk-down
 enabled by default).

CalibrationStatus.description:
Per-channel status of the latest calibration attempt. The UI polls
 `GET /calibrations/.../status` to drive its progress UI; the engine
 rewrites this entry on every sweep step and once more on the final
 terminal transition. The `Completed` and `Failed` variants stick
 around until the next diagnosis on the same channel resets it back
 to `InProgress`. `NotStarted` is returned when neither an in-memory
 snapshot nor a persisted calibration exists for the channel; the
 endpoint returns 200 in that case so the UI does not have to
 distinguish "channel unknown" from network errors via HTTP 404.

CalibrationView.description:
Calibration as exposed via the REST API. Flattens the persistence
 struct and adds derived fields the UI consumes.

down_curve.description: Down-sweep samples. Same shape, dense around the kick-in zone.

kick_boost_active.description:
Resolved kick-boost decision (override + heuristic). `true`
 when the dispatcher will apply the cold-start boost on the
 next Off->Kicking transition for this channel. Derived; the
 deserialize path is only used for JSON round-trips in tests
 and will be recomputed if `kick_boost_override` changes.

kick_boost_override.description:
User override for the cold-start kick boost. `None` defers to
 the heuristic (`needs_kick_boost`); `Some(true)` forces the
 boost on; `Some(false)` forces it off.

kick_duration_ms.description:
Measured time from writing `min_start_duty` to RPM crossing the
 start floor, plus a 50% safety margin. Used by the dispatcher's
 Off -> Kicking -> On hand-off. Bounded by the daemon's poll rate
 so values land on second-aligned increments.

kick_duration_override_ms.description:
User override for the kick-write duration. `None` uses the
 calibrated `kick_duration_ms`; `Some(ms)` forces the dispatcher
 to hold the kick for `ms` before dropping to sustain. Useful
 when a fan needs longer than the measured start-to-spin time to
 stabilize at the boosted kick duty.

max_eff_duty.description:
"Near plateau" duty (within `jitter` of `rpm_max`). Informational:
 `true_to_device(100)` can still write above this on fans that
 keep gaining RPM past it.

min_stable_duty.description:
Lowest device-duty without oscillation. Equals `min_sustain_duty`
 on healthy fans; sits above it on firmware-kicked fans that
 oscillate just above sustain (dispatcher clamps kick + sustain
 to this value).

min_start_duty.description: Lowest device-duty that reliably starts the fan from rest.

min_sustain_duty.description: Lowest device-duty that keeps the fan spinning once started.

up_curve.description: Up-sweep samples (duty 0..=100 ascending). Dense in kick-in, sparse above.

walk_after_kick_override.description:
User override for the post-kick walk-down. `None` defers to the
 default (walk down in small steps from kick to sustain); `Some(true)`
 forces the walk on; `Some(false)` jumps from kick straight to
 sustain. Skipping the walk is safe on hardware that tolerates an
 abrupt drop and removes a visible 1-22 second ramp on every cold
 start. Serialized with `#[serde(default)]` so calibrations written
 before this field existed deserialize cleanly to `None`.

warnings.description: Non-fatal reliability findings. Empty for a healthy calibration.

CalibrationWarning.description:
Non-fatal reliability findings. The calibration persists either way;
 these inform the popover and the completion notification.

oneOf.description:
Tachometer reported 0 RPM throughout: fan disconnected, tach
 wire unplugged, or firmware holding the fan off.

Fan spins but does not track duty (RPM span within noise). Most
 often BIOS/firmware-controlled. Mapping disabled (forced Stepped).

Usable RPM span is small enough that mapping resolution is coarse.
 Mapping stays active.

Fan never settled above `min_sustain_duty` (cheap firmware-kicked
 fan). `min_stable_duty` undefined; dispatcher falls back to
 `min_sustain_duty`. The range marks the observed oscillation band.

CalibrationsDto.description:
Snapshot of every persisted calibration. Empty list when nothing
 is stored. Matches the wrapper shape used by `/profiles` and
 `/alerts` so clients can iterate `dto.calibrations`.

ChannelExtensionNames.description:
Channel extension names that signal which `ChannelExtensions` are applicable
 for a particular device channel.

ChannelExtensions.description:
Device Channel specific settings
 This is used to store specialized settings (extensions) that apply to a specific device channel.

anyOf.description: Whether to use the device channel's internal hardware fan curve functionality.

Whether to use the AMDGPU RDNA3/4 features.
 It allows the device to run at zero RPM when the temperature is below a certain threshold.

hw_fan_curve_enabled.description:
Whether to use the internal HW Curve feature, instead of setting regular
 flat curves. Using this reduces functionality.

ChannelSource.description:
A source for displaying sensor data that is related to a particular channel.
 This is like `TempSource` but not limited to temperature sensors. (Load, Duty, etc.)

channel_name.description: The internal name for this channel source. NOT the Label.

device_uid.description: The associated device uid containing current values

ChannelStats.description:
Running min/max/avg/count for a single (channel, `data_type`) or temp pair.
 `count == 0` means no observation yet; min/max/avg are unused in that state.

allow_unencrypted.description: Allow unencrypted HTTP connections from non-localhost addresses

device_listener_enabled.description: Whether to listen for device add/remove events at startup

origins.description: Custom origins to allow in CORS (for reverse proxy setups)

protocol_header.description: Header to check for proxy client protocol (e.g., "X-Forwarded-Proto")

sensors_auto_detect.description: Whether to auto-detect Super-I/O sensors at startup (`x86_64` only)

children.description:
The Custom Sensor's children, if any.

 Each Custom Sensor is either a child, parent, or standalone, not a combination of those.
 Custom Sensors are limited to 1 level of hierarchy. This removes the possibility
 of circular references.

 The children and parents vectors are managed and filled internally. For GET endpoints,
 they provide this information for clients. For POST or PUT endpoints,
 any values here are essentially ignored.

id.description: ID MUST be unique, as `temp_name` must be unique.

parents.description: The Custom Sensor's parents, if any. See `children` for more details.

time_window_seconds.description:
The window in seconds over which a `TimeAverage` or `ExponentialMovingAvg` Custom
 Sensor smooths its source. Required for both, ignored for other types. Validated at
 the API boundary to be within `1..=300`.

DeviceExtensions.description:
Device specific extension settings
 This is used to store specialized settings (extensions) that apply to a specific device.
 More than one of these settings can be applied at a time.

delay_millis.description:
The delay in milliseconds to force between applying settings to this device.
 This is to help with communication issues with some devices that may not handle
 multiple settings applied in quick succession. (The driver does not always handle this)

direct_access.description:
Whether to enable Direct Access for the liquidctl driver,
 which will cause liquidctl to ignore the `HWMon` kernel driver

amd_gpu_overdrive.description:
When present, then this is an AMD RDNA3/4 GPU with PMFW fan curve support.
 True indicates overdrive is enabled and fan control is available.
 False indicates overdrive needs to be enabled via kernel boot parameter.

temp_max.description: The absolute maximum temp to use for Profiles for this device

temp_min.description: The absolute minimum temp to use for Profiles for this device

thinkpad_fan_control.description:
When present, then this is a `ThinkPad` device. True or False indicates whether Fan control
 is enabled for the kernel module and changing values is possible

status_history.description: Status history wrapped in Arc for O(1) cloning for serialization

locations.description:
If available various paths used to access the device.
 This can include paths like the kernel device path, hwmon path, HID path, or PCI Bus ID

name.description: If available the kernel driver name or liquidctl driver class.

version.description:
If available the driver's version.
 For kernel-based drivers this is the current kernel version.
 For liquidctl-based drivers this is the liquidctl version.
 For Nvidia-based drivers this is the version of the installed nvidia proprietary drivers.

DriverType.description: The Driver Type, or source of the driver actively being used for this device.

DutySample.description: A (device-duty, RPM) sample. Curves are sorted ascending by `duty`.

bypass_min_at_extremes.description:
Whether to bypass the minimum step size when the target duty is exactly 0% or 100%.
 Useful for ensuring fans fully stop or reach max RPM even when the change is below the
 minimum step size. The maximum step size is still respected. Disabled by default.

deviance.description: The temperature deviance threshold in degrees

duty_maximum.description:
The maximum duty change (step size) to apply
 A duty maximum of `0` indicates a fixed step size. Use `duty_minimum` to set the step size.
 Previously `duty_maximum`.

duty_minimum.description:
The minimum duty change (step size) to apply
 Previously `duty_minimum`.

name.description: The user given name for this function

only_downward.description: Whether to apply settings only on the way down

sample_window.description: The sample window this function should use, particularly applicable to moving averages

step_size_max_decreasing.description:
The maximum step size to apply when decreasing.
 A value of `0` indicates a fixed step size. Use `step_size_minimum_decreasing` to set the step size.

step_size_min_decreasing.description:
The minimum step size to apply when decreasing.
 A value of `0` indicates a symmetric step size. Use `duty_minimum` to set the step size.

threshold_hopping.description: Whether to temporarily bypass thresholds when fan speed remains unchanged for 30+ seconds to meet curve target.

uid.description: The Unique identifier for this function

driver_type.description: An Enum representation of the various Liquidctl driver classes

firmware_version.description: The detected firmware version at initialization

unknown_asetek.description: An indicator for needed user input to determine actual asetek690lc device

LcdCarouselSettings.description:
Settings for the LCD Carousel.

 This can be used to have a carousel of images (static or gif), of sensor data,
 or a combination of both.

images_path.description:
The absolute path directory location for images for the carousel. All applicable images
 present are processed when the setting is applied.

interval.description: The interval in seconds (2-900) in which to change images in the carousel.

colors.description: a list of RGB tuple values, eg [(20,20,120), (0,0,255)]

image_file_processed.description: The LCD Image processed file path location, where the preprocessed image is located.

temp_source.description: A temp source for displaying a temperature.

colors.description: a list of RGB tuple values, eg [(20,20,120), (0,0,255)]

function_uid.description: The function uid to apply to this profile

member_profile_uids.description: The profiles that make up the mix profile

mix_function_type.description: The function to mix the members with if this is a Mix Profile

name.description: The User given name for this Profile

offset_profile.description:
The graph offset to apply to the associated member profile
 This can also be used as a static offset when there is only one duty/offset pair.

speed_fixed.description: The fixed duty speed to set. eg: 20 (%)

speed_profile.description: The profile temp/duty speeds to set. eg: [(20.0, 50), (25.7, 80)]

temp_max.description: The maximum temp for this profile

temp_min.description: The minimum temp for this profile

uid.description: The Unique Identifier for this Profile

Setting.description:
Setting is used to store applied Settings to a device channel.
 These are the general core settings that apply to a wide range of device and channel types.
 Specialized settings are stored in `DeviceExtensions` and `ChannelExtensions`.
 Only one specific lighting or speed setting is applied to a specific channel at a time.

profile_uid.description: The Profile UID that applies to this device channel

reset_to_default.description: Used to set hwmon & nvidia channels back to their default 'automatic' values.

speed_fixed.description: The fixed duty speed to set. eg: 20 (%)

extension.description: If present, then this channel has special settings that are applicable.

fixed_enabled.description: True if manual fan speed control is supported; if false, speeds are read-only (monitoring only).

max_duty.description: The maximum fan duty for this speed channel

min_duty.description: The minimum fan duty for this speed channel

backend.description:
Which backend to use. Omit for the daemon's default
 (stress-ng if installed, else built-in).

duration_secs.description: Duration in seconds. Defaults to 60, max 600.

thread_count.description: Number of threads. Defaults to all available CPU cores.

backend.description: Which backend to use. Omit for the daemon's default (built-in).

duration_secs.description: Duration in seconds. Defaults to 60, max 600.

threads.description: Number of I/O threads. Defaults to 4.

backend.description: Which backend to use. Omit for the daemon's default (built-in).

duration_secs.description: Duration in seconds. Defaults to 60, max 600.

backend.description:
Which backend to use. Omit for the daemon's default
 (stress-ng if installed, else built-in).

duration_secs.description: Duration in seconds. Defaults to 60, max 600.

StatsResponse.description:
Top-level response for `GET /stats` and `DELETE /stats`. One entry per
 known device. Channels/temps are only present once they have been
 observed at least once since daemon start (or last reset).

Status.description: A Model which contains various applicable device statuses

StressBackend.description: Which backend is running (or will run) a stress test.

device_uid.description: The associated device uid containing current temp values

temp_name.description: The internal name for this Temperature Source. NOT the `TempInfo` Label.
